feed.title: PQSafe AgentPay
feed.subtitle: Post-quantum signed SpendEnvelopes for AI agent payments — spec updates, SDK releases, and standards progress.

entry.title: Real ML-DSA-65 verifier deployed at api.pqsafe.xyz
entry.summary: For six weeks, /v1/mandates/verify accepted any signature payload. Today the stub is gone — replaced by a real mldsa65.verify() call from @noble/post-quantum. 3,309-byte ML-DSA-65 signature validation, SHA-256(JCS) fingerprint protocol, and six published conformance test vectors. Anyone can run npx @pqsafe/conformance against the live endpoint.
entry.content:
For six weeks, our /v1/mandates/verify endpoint returned 200 OK for any signature payload. Today that stub is gone. The endpoint now calls mldsa65.verify() from @noble/post-quantum on every request.

Three concrete things landed in production: real signature verification (3,309-byte ML-DSA-65), public key length check (1,952 bytes), and SHA-256(JCS) fingerprint protocol. Six conformance test vectors published at pqsafe.xyz/spec/ap2-pq-test-vectors-v1.json — including TC-N1 which exposes a silent-accept bug in pqcrypto 0.4.0.

Run: npx @pqsafe/conformance --endpoint https://api.pqsafe.xyz/v1/mandates/verify
